text:
Here's what we learned from the multi-turn capture:

**Turn 1** (#001-#010): 7 messages, 1 subagent — explored the workspace
**Turn 2** (#011-#034+): 1,194+ messages — the full turn 1 history was resent on every API call, plus new tool calls. Heavy Bash usage (counting lines in every file)
**Turn 3** (#035-#110): failed with exit code 1 — likely hit context limits or token budget. By this point each API call was sending 1,200+ messages

The key insight: **multi-turn conversations get expensive fast.** The message array grows with every turn, and since the entire history is resent each iteration, turn 3 was sending the entire turn 1 + turn 2 history on every API call. That's why it eventually failed — and why compaction exists.
